setups.name: VCP
setups.one_liner: A multi-week base where each successive pullback is shallower than the last and volume dries up at the final contraction, ending in a breakout above the pivot on expanding volume.
key_criteria: ≥20% gain in the 3 months before the base (ideally 30–50%+)
2–6 contractions (typically 3–4), each shallower than the last
Rightmost contraction volume noticeably below base average
Highest high of the rightmost contraction
Close above pivot on volume ≥1.5× 50-day avg
aplus_filters: Final contraction weekly range <5% in last 1–2 weeks before breakout
Breakout day volume ≥2× 50-day avg (not just ≥1.5×)
Breakout close in upper 50% of the day's range
Within 5% of 52-week high at the pivot
RS line at a new high leading price into the pivot
management: Stop: 7–8% below pivot, or below rightmost-contraction low (tighter wins)
Max stop width ≤ 1× ADR(20) at entry
Failure: close below pivot on expanding volume within 5 days
8-week hold rule: if +20% in ≤3 weeks, hold 8 weeks minimum
Never enter more than 5% above the pivot

setups.name: Cup with Handle
setups.one_liner: A smooth U-shaped cup (no sharp V) followed by a small downward-drifting handle in the upper half of the cup, with breakout above the handle's resistance on volume.
key_criteria: Smooth rounded U (NOT a sharp V). Multiple weeks of sideways basing at the bottom.
≤33% of the prior advance (up to 50% in bear-adjacent markets)
≤33% of the cup's rally off the low (ideally 10–15%)
Slight downward tilt preferred (shakes out weak hands)
MUST form in upper half of cup (lower half = Low Cheat, not C&H)
aplus_filters: Handle forms above the 50-day SMA at its low (stronger support)
Volume dries up inside the handle (weaker than cup's right side)
Cup duration 3–6 months (not the 7-week minimum)
Stock within 15% of 52-week high at the handle low
Base is 1st or 2nd stage (3rd/4th fail ~80%)
Breakout day closes in the top 25% of the day's range
Pre-breakout tightness: final week <4% weekly range
management: Stop: 7–8% below pivot, or below the handle low
Buy within 5% of the pivot — extended entries fail more often
Failure: close below the handle low within 10 days of breakout
8-week hold rule applies

setups.name: Flat Base
setups.one_liner: A tight sideways consolidation ≤15% deep lasting 5+ weeks, following a ≥30% prior advance. Horizontal, not sloping. Institutions holding tight; minimal selling pressure.
key_criteria: Horizontal or slight downward drift (NOT ascending)
Both top and bottom of range touched 2+ times each
aplus_filters: Base depth ≤10% (not just ≤15%)
Volume dries up progressively during consolidation
50 SMA rising through the base
Strongest: stock doesn't even retest the range low before breakout
Darvas Box variant: ≥2 touches on each boundary
management: Stop: below the range low (which defines the base bottom)
Buy within 3% of the pivot — shallow bases, extended entries destroy R:R
Failure: close below 50 SMA within 5 days of breakout

setups.name: Double Bottom
setups.one_liner: A W-shaped base with two distinct lows separated by a 10–20% intervening rally, where the second low ideally undercuts the first slightly (shakeout), followed by breakout above the middle peak on expanding volume.
key_criteria: Minimum 7 weeks from left edge to pivot (4+ weeks between lows)
Initial trough after downtrend or shallow correction
Within 3% of first low (at, slightly above, or slightly below)
Second low UNDERCUTS first slightly (1–5%) — shakeout is positive
Break above the MIDDLE PEAK (not the second low) — critical distinction
1st low high vol → rally low vol → 2nd low low vol → breakout high vol
aplus_filters: Second low undercuts first by 1–3% then reverses within 1–3 days (Wyckoff Spring)
CMF/OBV/A-D improving through second decline (accumulation signal)
Time between lows: 4–12 weeks (shorter = stronger)
Middle peak rally ≥15% (stronger than 10% minimum)
Part of a sector rotation setup (peers also bottoming)
Breakout gaps up through middle peak resistance
management: Stop: 7–8% below pivot or below 2nd low (whichever tighter)
Re-entry: pullback to test middle-peak-turned-support
Target: distance from middle peak to lows, added to pivot
Failure: close below 2nd low after breakout = pattern invalidated

setups.name: HTF
setups.one_liner: A stock that explodes ≥100% in 4–8 weeks (the pole), then consolidates in a shallow flag ≤25% deep for 3–5 weeks with volume drying up, then breaks out. The rarest and most explosive bull pattern.
key_criteria: Massive volume on the advance (way above normal)
3–5 weeks (can be as short as 10–12 days)
Must contract progressively — VCP-like dry-up within flag
aplus_filters: Pole ≥100% in ≤6 weeks (not full 8)
Flag ≤15% deep (not 20–25% max)
1–2 ultra-low-volume days before breakout (“squat”)
Flag stays entirely above 10 EMA / 21 EMA
Only pattern Minervini enters without fundamentals — doubling in 8 weeks on massive volume telegraphs something fundamental
management: Stop: below the flag low or just below pivot (typically tighter than standard 7–8%)
After +2R to +3R profit, move stop to breakeven
Hold aggressively — multi-bagger candidates
Failure: close below flag low after breakout

setups.name: Low Cheat
setups.source: Mark Minervini (Trade Like a Stock Market Wizard, p.243)
setups.one_liner: An earlier entry technique: a tight, low-volume consolidation forms in the LOWER-to-MIDDLE third of a base (not the upper third handle), creating a “mini-pivot” that triggers before the full base pivot.
key_criteria: A forming base (typically a cup), not yet broken out
Lower or middle third of base (upper third = regular handle)
Near the LOWEST in the entire base (seller exhaustion)
aplus_filters: VCP characteristics inside the cheat zone
Cheat forms right at or just above 50 SMA
Cheat range ≤5% (not 10% max)
1–2 dry-up volume days before mini-pivot break
Surrounding base already shows VCP qualities
management: PILOT position, not a full entry. “Entry at the earliest point, not your entire position.”
Scaling: pilot at cheat → standard add at full base pivot → optional 3rd on confirmation
Stop: below the cheat consolidation low (very tight)
Each add at higher prices, never average down

setups.name: IPO Base
setups.one_liner: The first proper base formed within 1–3 years of a stock going public. Unique rules: wider depth tolerance, shorter duration minimum.
key_criteria: Post-IPO rally (stock didn't go straight down)
Weak holders from IPO allocation finish selling
aplus_filters: FIRST base after IPO (not 2nd or 3rd)
IPO lockup expiration has passed (typically 90–180 days post-IPO)
Institutional ownership appearing in quarterly filings
Revenue/earnings growth >25% YoY since IPO
Relative strength in top 20–30% of all stocks
Base depth ≤33% (not 50% max)
Sector-level strength
management: Stop: 7–10% below pivot (slightly wider for IPO volatility)
Size down — volatility is higher than standard bases
Failure: break of base low on volume

setups.name: Ascending Base
setups.one_liner: A consolidation formed by three distinct 10–20% pullbacks, each with a HIGHER low than the previous. Stock is too strong to form a normal base.
key_criteria: Three pullbacks, each bottoming at a higher low
Each subsequent low measurably higher than previous
Each subsequent high equals or exceeds previous
9–16 weeks from left edge to pivot
Break above 3rd (rightmost) high on expanding volume
aplus_filters: Each pullback shallower than last (10 → 15 → 12% with rising lows is fine)
Volume dries up on each pullback, expands on each rally
Stock stays above 50 SMA throughout
Tight action in final 1–2 weeks before breakout
Breakout from very tight final swing high
management: Stop: below the most recent (3rd) low — tight because lows rise
Continuation pattern — stock already strong, breakout runs fast
Failure: close below 3rd low

setups.name: Base-on-Base
setups.one_liner: A stock breaks out from a first base, runs 15–25% (insufficient for a full cup), then consolidates into a SECOND base on top of the first. Staircase shape. Very bullish — institutions accumulating continuously.
key_criteria: Any valid base (cup/flat/VCP) that already produced a breakout
15–25% gain (NOT 30%+ which would start a new cup)
5–15% from the high of the run
Second base entirely ABOVE the first base's top
aplus_filters: Second base depth ≤10%
Very shallow pullback from inter-base high
Volume dry-up in the second base
Combined B-on-B duration >12 weeks (long consolidation = strong accumulation)
First base was already a high-quality pattern
management: Stop: below the SECOND base low (1st base low too far)
Treat as continuation of first breakout's advance
Failure: close below the first base's high

setups.name: Pocket Pivot
setups.one_liner: An up-day where volume exceeds the highest down-day volume of the prior 10 sessions, occurring at/near a key moving average — detecting institutional accumulation BEFORE a standard breakout would trigger.
key_criteria: That day's volume > highest volume on ANY down day in prior 10 sessions
Stock “up and off” or “up and through” the 10-day or 50-day MA
Stock has been respecting the 50 SMA during prior advance
aplus_filters: Occurs inside a constructive base (pre-breakout PP)
2–3 pocket pivots within a few sessions — clustered institutional buying
Bounce is measured, not parabolic (no V-recovery)
Not extended from 10 SMA (<5% above)
Day closes in top 25% of range
RS line making new highs or at least flat
disqualifiers: 5+ month downtrend — wait for rounding base
Below both 50 SMA and 200 SMA
V-shaped recovery (sharp selloff → straight back up through MAs)
Rising wedge pattern
Close in lower 1/3 of daily range
Extended from base AND from 10 SMA
management: Stop: below the MA being tested or below the PP day's low
Max stop 7–8% from entry
Seven-Week Rule: if stock holds above 10 SMA for ≥7 weeks after PP, use 10 SMA violation as sell signal; otherwise 50 SMA
Violation test (two-part): close below MA + next day breaks that day's intraday low

setups.name: Pullback to MA
setups.one_liner: Buying a constructive low-volume pullback to a rising key moving average (10 EMA / 20 EMA / 50 SMA) in a stock in a confirmed Stage 2 uptrend. Follow-on entry technique, not a standalone big-winner trigger.
key_criteria: Above rising 50 SMA, Stage 2 intact, MAs stacked (10 EMA > 20 EMA > 50 SMA)
Price touches or slightly undercuts MA, then closes back above
Drying up — expanding volume = distribution, skip
Close back above MA, ideally on a reversal candle
aplus_filters: FIRST touch is best (O'Neil's Three Strikes rule: 1st=best, 2nd=OK, 3rd=often fails)
Pullback duration 1–3 weeks (quick and orderly)
MAs stay stacked (no convergence/crossing)
RS line holds up during pullback
Pullback depth <5% (very tight)
Prior advance ≥30%
management: Stop: below MA being tested or below pullback low
Follow-on add is 50–75% of a full initial position
Never average down
Failure: close below 50 SMA on volume

setups.name: Break & Retest
setups.one_liner: Instead of buying the breakout, wait for price to pull back and retest the former resistance (now support), then enter on the successful retest. Tighter stop, better R:R, avoids fakeouts.
key_criteria: Above a major level in the preceding 1–15 sessions
≥2 confirming factors (prior high, MA, round number, prior breakout level)
Price pulls back to within 1–3% of former resistance, holds, bounces
Light volume on pullback (no heavy selling)
Reversal candle at/above the level, or break of retest consolidation high
aplus_filters: Level has 3+ confluence factors
Retest within 5–10 days of original breakout (fresh, not stale)
Retest low barely undercuts level, or doesn't reach it
Broader market in uptrend
Low-volume inside day during retest = institutional accumulation
management: Target: recent swing high from initial breakout
Fail: close below retest level on expanding volume

setups.name: Undercut & Rally
setups.one_liner: A stock briefly breaks a well-defined support level (triggering stop losses), then quickly reclaims the level within 1–5 days. The failed breakdown becomes the entry — stops flushed, institutions absorb supply, stock rallies with less overhead.
key_criteria: Clear, obvious level with 2+ prior touches
Stock recovers above broken level within 1–5 days
Stage 2 intact (150 SMA > 200 SMA, 200 SMA rising)
Close above the reclaimed level, on volume
aplus_filters: Undercut at the END of a base (final shakeout before breakout)
Reclaim within 1–2 days (not 5)
Volume on reclaim > volume on undercut
Undercut depth 1–2% (just enough to trigger stops)
Stock never closes materially below the level on a daily basis
RS line holds up through the undercut
management: Stop: below the undercut low (very tight — new line in the sand)
If undercut low breaks again, exit immediately
Failure: no recovery after 5+ days, or follow-through selling

setups.name: Episodic Pivot
setups.one_liner: A stock gaps up ≥5% on a fundamental catalyst (earnings, FDA, contract, guidance) with ≥2.5× average volume. Entry comes AFTER the gap, buying the first constructive pullback or the breakout of the 3–10 day post-gap consolidation.
key_criteria: Holds above gap day midpoint (stronger) or gap day low (acceptable)
Tight 3–10 day consolidation after gap day
Buy first pullback holding above gap low, OR buy breakout of post-gap range
aplus_filters: Gap ≥15% + volume ≥5×
Already in uptrend before the gap (not deep-value turnaround)
Post-gap range very tight (inside days)
Sector alignment — peers moving on same catalyst type
Gap fills <30% in first 2 days (strong hands holding)
ADR% ≥5% post-gap (enough range to trade)
disqualifiers: No identifiable catalyst
Gap fills below prior close within 2 days
Volume declining rapidly after gap day
Gap into prior resistance
Stock already extended before gap (exhaustion gap)
management: Stop: below gap day's low or post-gap consolidation low
Failure: gap fills below prior close = failed EP, exit

setups.name: Gap & Go
setups.one_liner: Same family as EP, but entry happens on Day 1 (the gap day itself), not after consolidation. Higher risk, higher reward. Requires ≥10% gap and ≥3× volume.
key_criteria: Holds above open, or recovers quickly (not “gap and crap”)
During/after opening range (first 60 min), on VWAP pullback with volume
aplus_filters: Gap ≥20% (not just 10%)
Volume ≥5×
Prior basing in place (gap from consolidation)
Float <50M shares (smaller float = more explosive)
Tight post-open range (buyers absorbing, not dumping)
Sector in favor
disqualifiers: Gap fills below prior close within first hour
High volume selling immediately after open
No identifiable catalyst (possible manipulation)
Already extended before the gap
After-hours faded most of the gap before the open
management: Stop: below gap day low or below VWAP
Time-based: enter within first 60 minutes
Abnormal Gap Reversal (180°): gap DOWN on bad news that immediately engulfs + reclaims 10/20 MA

setups.name: Parabolic Short
setups.one_liner: A stock that has gone parabolic (extended ≥40% above 10 EMA, ≥60% above 20 EMA) and shows exhaustion signals (volume climax + doji/shooting star). Mean-reversion short.
key_criteria: Recent day ≥3× avg volume + doji/shooting star candle
Above the recent high (non-negotiable — parabolics squeeze violently)
aplus_filters: Momentum divergence: RSI making lower highs while price makes higher highs
Market in broad weakness
Stock has history of parabolic-then-crash
disqualifiers: Strong fundamental catalyst supporting the move
Broad market in strong uptrend
Short squeeze mechanics (high SI, low float, gamma exposure)

setups.name: Structural Short
setups.one_liner: Shorting former bull market leaders transitioning from Stage 3 (topping) into Stage 4 (decline). Requires downward-trending 50 SMA, Stage 3 duration ≥1 month, and price breaking below 200 SMA.
key_criteria: Price breaking below or trading under
Decisive break below 200 SMA or key support
After breakdown, stock rallies to test underside of 50/200 MA and fails
management: Order: Buy Stop GTC only (never Buy Stop Limit)
Standard stop 8%, max 10%
Portfolio: max 25–33.3% total short exposure
Double Entry: 1/3–1/2 pilot at breakdown, add on failed-retest 2nd entry
Profit target: 18–25% for major stocks, cover at least half

setups.name: Retail Exhaustion
setups.one_liner: Small-cap short strategy: identify when retail buying power is mechanically exhausted by tracking total dollar volume against market-cap thresholds, or trap bag holders on a catalyst-driven bounce.
aplus_filters: First meaningful red day after a multi-day run = reversal beginning
Dux's data: average subsequent move -26.4%
disqualifiers: BLACKLISTED: Biotech. 10 years net-flat results. Irrational due to binary catalysts.
